title: OpenAI Launches GPT-5.5, Its Most Capable Agentic Model Yet
summary: OpenAI released GPT-5.5 on April 23, positioning it as the company's smartest and most agentic model, built to plan, use tools, and complete complex tasks with minimal human intervention.
content: OpenAI launched GPT-5.5 on April 23, 2026, calling it its 'smartest and most intuitive to use model yet' and the next step toward 'a new way of getting work done on a computer.' The model excels at writing and debugging code, online research, creating spreadsheets and documents, and executing complex multi-step tasks without constant human supervision — users can hand off messy, multi-part tasks and trust GPT-5.5 to plan, use tools, check its work, and navigate ambiguity independently. GPT-5.5 is the first fully retrained base model since GPT-4.5, co-designed with NVIDIA's GB200 and GB300 NVL72 rack-scale hardware to sustain long-running agentic workflows at scale. It rolls out to Plus, Pro, Business, and Enterprise ChatGPT users and Codex, with API access priced at $5 per million input tokens and $30 per million output tokens. OpenAI chief scientist Jakub Pachocki noted that the last two years of model progress had felt 'surprisingly slow,' signaling that this release marks a meaningful acceleration in agentic AI capability.

title: Anthropic Launches Claude Security to Help Enterprises Find and Fix Code Vulnerabilities
summary: Anthropic released Claude Security in public beta, a dedicated tool powered by Claude Opus 4.7 that scans enterprise codebases for vulnerabilities and generates targeted patches.
content: Anthropic launched Claude Security in public beta on April 30, 2026, enabling enterprise security teams to scan codebases for vulnerabilities and receive AI-generated patches without needing API integration or custom agent builds. Powered by Claude Opus 4.7, users simply select a repository, directory, or branch, trigger a scan, and receive findings complete with confidence ratings, severity assessments, exploitability analysis, and step-by-step remediation instructions. Since a research preview in February, hundreds of organizations have used the tool to discover exploits that existing security tools had missed for years, and it is already reducing days of back-and-forth between security and engineering teams to a single working session. Anthropic added scheduled scans for continuous coverage, dismissal notes for audit trails, and Markdown and CSV exports for integration into existing audit systems. The product also ships with integrations from major security platforms including CrowdStrike, Microsoft Security, Palo Alto Networks, SentinelOne, TrendAI, and Wiz, which are embedding Claude Opus 4.7 directly into the security platforms enterprises already run.

title: Google Rolls Out Gemini AI Assistant to Millions of Cars via Software Update
summary: Google announced Gemini is replacing Google Assistant in cars with Google built-in, with General Motors confirming the rollout across approximately 4 million existing GM vehicles.
content: Google announced on April 30, 2026 that it will begin rolling out Gemini to all cars with Google built-in, replacing the existing Google Assistant and enabling a far more natural, conversational driving experience. The rollout closely follows General Motors confirming Gemini access across approximately 4 million vehicles from model year 2022 and newer — including Cadillac, Chevrolet, Buick, and GMC brands — through over-the-air software updates rather than requiring new vehicle purchases. With Gemini, drivers can engage in multi-turn conversations to find restaurants with specific features along a route, handle follow-up questions about parking or menus, manage vehicle controls, get directions, and respond to messages hands-free. A Gemini Live beta also enables open-ended real-time discussions while driving, activated by saying 'Hey Google, let's talk.' Google plans to expand Gemini to additional languages, regions, and integrations with Gmail, Google Calendar, and Google Home in future updates.

title: OpenAI and AWS Expand Partnership to Bring GPT-5.5 and Codex to Amazon Bedrock
summary: OpenAI and Amazon Web Services expanded their strategic partnership, making GPT-5.5, Codex, and Managed Agents available on Amazon Bedrock for the first time.
content: OpenAI and AWS announced a major expansion of their strategic partnership on April 28, 2026, making OpenAI's frontier models including GPT-5.5, the Codex coding agent suite, and a new Managed Agents service available on Amazon Bedrock in limited preview. Enterprises can now access OpenAI capabilities within their existing AWS environments, applying them against the security controls, identity systems, compliance frameworks, and cloud commitments they already rely on. Codex — now used by more than 4 million people weekly for coding, refactoring, documentation, and analysis workflows — can be deployed via Bedrock with eligible usage counting toward AWS cloud spend commitments. The new Amazon Bedrock Managed Agents service, powered by OpenAI, handles the hardest parts of enterprise agent deployment including tool use, orchestration, context management, and governance, providing a governed path from prototype to production. The deal is strategically significant as it marks OpenAI's first major multi-cloud expansion beyond its primary partnership with Microsoft's Azure.

title: Google DeepMind Launches AI Co-Clinician Research Initiative for Healthcare
summary: Google DeepMind announced an AI co-clinician initiative that pairs Gemini with clinical supervision to support patient consultations, diagnosis, and care planning.
content: Google DeepMind announced its AI co-clinician research initiative on April 30, 2026, exploring how AI can function as a collaborative member of clinical care teams working under expert physician supervision. Built on Gemini and Project Astra, the system uses live audio and video during simulated telemedical calls to engage with patients and assist clinicians with diagnosis, medication reasoning, and care planning — moving beyond text-based AI into real-time multimodal medical consultation. In evaluations covering 140 aspects of consultation skill, AI co-clinician performed at a level comparable to or exceeding primary care physicians in 68 areas, though expert physicians still outperformed the system overall, particularly in identifying critical 'red flags' and guiding physical examinations. DeepMind is advancing phased research collaborations in the U.S., India, Australia, New Zealand, Singapore, and UAE, framing the tool as a supportive resource for practitioners rather than a replacement for clinical judgment. A full technical report, 'Towards Conversational Medical AI with Eyes, Ears and a Voice,' has been published alongside the announcement.

title: DeepMind's David Silver Raises $1.1B for AI Startup That Learns Without Human Data
summary: Ineffable Intelligence, founded by DeepMind's David Silver, raised $1.1 billion at a $5.1 billion valuation to build a 'superlearner' AI that acquires knowledge purely through reinforcement learning.
content: Ineffable Intelligence, a British AI lab founded months ago by former DeepMind researcher David Silver, raised $1.1 billion in funding at a $5.1 billion valuation in late April 2026, joining a wave of pedigree-led AI startups attracting massive early-stage rounds. Silver, who led DeepMind's reinforcement learning team and co-developed AlphaZero — which mastered chess and Go without any human training data — aims to build a 'superlearner' capable of discovering all knowledge and skills purely from its own experience, with no dependence on human-generated datasets. The round was led by Sequoia Capital and Lightspeed Venture Partners, with participation from Index Ventures, Google, Nvidia, the British Business Bank, and Sovereign AI, the U.K.'s newly launched sovereign venture fund. The raise follows AMI Labs ($1.03B, co-founded by Yann LeCun) and Recursive Superintelligence ($500M–$1B, co-founded by Tim Rocktäschel), pointing to a surge in London as an emerging AI research hub alongside Silicon Valley. Silver described the project as his life's work, pledging any personal profits to high-impact charities.
